mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below its trailing 12-month average signals persistently accommodative monetary policy, which lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS deviation from trailing average and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth (RSAFS) and nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a self-reinforcing consumption-employment cycle that supports sustained corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; neither series alone is sufficient because payroll growth without spending growth reflects labor hoarding, not demand strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1.5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to outperform single-series triggers by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In periods where the federal funds rate exceeds trailing headline CPI inflation (implying a positive real policy rate), further increases in FEDFUNDS have a materially stronger negative effect on equity valuations than in negative real-rate regimes, because restrictive real rates directly compress risk premia; the same FEDFUNDS rise in a negative real-rate regime is largely absorbed without equity impact.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL YoY inflation > 0
failure_condition: Negative real-rate regime periods show equally strong negative return sensitivity to FEDFUNDS increases as positive real-rate regime periods (difference in slope coefficients not significant at p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects near-term strengthening in manufacturing output that is quickly priced by markets, producing a short-lived positive S&P 500 return drift over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO month-over-month change falls below 0.02 Rank IC over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% from its trailing 12-month peak signals an impending contraction in interest-rate-sensitive demand that historically leads broader economic slowdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST decline episodes are followed by positive median 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) acts as a continuous coincident indicator of economic momentum; higher growth rates are associated with modestly higher near-term S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets incrementally price in stronger consumer income.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.03 across all rolling 2-year windows tested.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high real GDP growth (GDPC1 above its trailing 4-quarter average), rising federal funds rates are tolerated by equity markets because they reflect confidence in economic strength rather than emergency tightening, muting the typically negative rate-hike effect; in low-growth regimes the same rate increases produce sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns because they compound growth concerns.
failure_condition: The differential 20-day return response to FEDFUNDS hikes between high-growth and low-growth GDPC1 regimes is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) across a 5-year rolling sample.
